text.title: Solo Niche Disqualifier Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Solo Niche Disqualifier Checklist application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Freelancer MUST maintain ≥5 named industries / project types on a hard-no list; leads matching any item are rejected without a call.
rule.rationale: Without a pre-written list every lead lands in discovery and erodes capacity.

rule.statement: Cold-lead evaluation MUST be ≤5 minutes; longer evaluations migrate decision quality into politeness.
rule.rationale: Solo operators systematically under-budget evaluation and over-budget polite engagement; the cap converts fuzzy into deterministic.

rule.statement: Leads stating or implying a budget below the floor MUST be rejected; no counter-offer at the floor.

rationale.source: Jonathan Stark — Hourly Billing Is Nuts

rule.rationale: Counter-offers signal flexibility and invite further downward negotiation.

rule.statement: Brief MUST name the decision-maker (title or person); 'we' / 'the team' / 'I'll check with co-founder' briefs are rejected as not-yet-ready.
rule.rationale: Multi-stakeholder briefs convert <5% and consume 3x more discovery time.

rule.statement: Rejection reply MUST NOT propose alternative pricing/scope/future-fit window; counter-offers reopen negotiation.

rationale.source: Jonathan Stark — Hourly Billing Is Nuts

rule.rationale: Counter-offers re-introduce the leak the filter exists to close.
